title: Artillery2 Tutorial Part 3
description: Learn more about the Artillery2 VST/AU Action Effect! Tweak your tracks in realtime, create crazy sound designs, with Artillery2 you get it on the spot fast and easy.
transcript: now that we have a basic understanding of how artillery Works let's take a closer look at some effects this time we'll open a global preset called empty so we can start from scratch click and drag above the keyboard to create a zone now we'll drop in the Looper which you can find under the granular category of effects the first thing I'll do is set the keyboard input quanti to eighth notes so that the loop is triggered in sync with the sound coming in even with my sloppy timing here the loop size depends on which note I play on the keyboard you can see the size parameter change as I play different notes that's because the key track modulation is turned up all the way to the right notice the Lower Keys play faster loops and the high Keys play slower Loops to reverse this we'll change the size setting which is the basis value for our modulation to a half note instead of a 30second note then we'll crank the key track knob all the way to the left reversing the direction of modulation to really mash up a drum Loop creatively see the universal modulator we'll choose a square wave to add more modulation to the size parameter of the loop the step Looper is a great variation of classic Loop in this effect steps forward through the audio material at a rate determined by the size and count parameter settings in this case the loop size is a cordin note and the loop will play four times before moving on to the next Loop point to get an idea of some of the other loop effects in artillery load the global preset called Loopers paradise and play around a bit a classic must-have effect for many styles is the vocoder as with all other effects in artillery the Innovative mix envelope handles the proportion of dry to wet signal in the output you can change the mix amount by just dragging the envelope up or down must have effect for many but there's also an attack and release component that can be Tempo synced the 41 setting here means that it takes four full measures for the effect amount to go from 0 to 100% AIC likewise going from 100% back to zero lasts a half note plus you can shape the attack release envelopes by clicking and dragging on their handles a classic must have effect for many in the next video we'll look at some more modulation possibilities with artillery thanks for watching
